metadata.summary: Decompose top-line goals into 2-4 product outcomes with 4-week leading indicators and confidence levels, then generate four synchronized audience views from one source of truth.

content.title: Outcome-Based Roadmaps Advanced
content.summary: Decompose a top-line business goal into 2-4 product outcomes, each with a leading indicator measurable within 4 weeks, a confidence level (low/med/high) backed by evidence, and 2-3 candidate experiments. Maintain one source-of-truth roadmap, then generate four audience views from it: theme-based (customers, no dates), outcome-metric (board, quarterly targets), outcome-plus-options (engineering, sprint-level when known), and problem-solution (sales, only confirmed items get timing). Confidence inflation — defaulting to "medium" without evidence — is the primary failure mode.

why.p: Stakeholders who demand feature timelines despite an outcome-first culture are solving a communication problem, not a roadmap-format problem. Audience-tailored views from a single source of truth provide the narrative each audience needs without creating four diverging documents. Breaking a business goal into product outcomes with leading indicators is the highest-leverage PM skill for 2026: it forces explicit evidence requirements and surfaces "what we still need to learn" before committing to solutions.

list.item: Stakeholders keep demanding feature timelines; you need audience-tailored views per cohort (board, sales, eng, customers).
Translating a top-line business goal into a tree of product outcomes with leading indicators.
Quarterly re-planning where confidence levels shift and the same roadmap must be presented to different audiences.
A PM transitioning from feature owner to "architect of impact" who needs scaffolding for the new role.
Trigger phrase: agent or human says "we need a roadmap that says when feature X ships" while leadership has formally adopted outcome-based planning.
Early-stage product (pre-PMF) where outcomes are unstable and discovery dominates — use a "now/next/later" theme list.
Single-stakeholder context (just you and your co-founder) — the audience-matrix overhead has zero ROI.
Enterprise with hard-deadline contractual commitments — use a hybrid (outcome theme plus dated milestones).
Org with no metrics infrastructure — you cannot operate confidence levels without measurement.

how-to.step: Collect inputs. Gather the single business goal (one sentence with a number and a date), the named audiences (default: customers, board, engineering, sales), the metrics layer access (warehouse, BI URL, query language), and the kill-criteria appetite (how much movement counts as "moving"). If any input is missing, stop and ask the human PM.

Draft the outcome tree. Decompose the goal into exactly 2-4 product outcomes. For each outcome write: outcome statement (in user-impact voice, not feature voice), leading indicator (must be observable inside 4 weeks), evidence-we-have (link to query/dashboard or "TO BE INSTRUMENTED"), what-we-still-need-to-learn, 2-3 candidate experiments, confidence level (low/medium/high) with mandatory evidence pointer, and a kill criterion ("if X does not move by Y by date Z, drop this outcome"). Save to source-of-truth file (`roadmap.yaml`).
Validate the outcome tree against the schema and the cap rules. Run `python templates/validate-outcome-tree.py roadmap.yaml`. Fix every reported issue before generating any audience view.

Generate four audience views from the source-of-truth file. Use the audience-rewriter prompt below. Save outputs as `views/customers.md`, `views/board.md`, `views/engineering.md`, `views/sales.md`. Never edit a view directly; always regenerate from the source.
Run the view-drift diff. Diff each view against the source and flag any line in a view without a source-of-truth row id. Fail the build if drift exists.

Schedule cadence. Weekly: leading-indicator monitor pulls metrics and proposes promote/demote/replace per outcome. Quarterly: re-decompose the entire goal tree from scratch. Re-version the roadmap (bump SemVer minor) only on real signal — a sustained metric move greater than 10% of the kill threshold for two consecutive weeks.

Hand off. Human PM signs off on outcome decomposition before any audience view is published. Customer-facing view requires human edit before publication to strip jargon. Sales view publishes only items with confidence=high.

rule.statement: Decompose one business goal into exactly 2-4 product outcomes. More than 4 outcomes from a single goal signals lack of focus and must be collapsed.
rule.rationale: Outcome trees with 6-8 branches dilute team attention and make experiment planning incoherent. Hard cap at 4 forces strategic prioritization before committing to measurement.

rule.statement: Every outcome must have a leading indicator measurable within 4 weeks. An outcome whose leading indicator requires more than 4 weeks to observe cannot support fast learning.
rule.rationale: Slow leading indicators mean the team runs for a quarter before knowing if the outcome is moving. The 4-week constraint forces selection of leading (not lagging) metrics.

rule.statement: Every outcome must list 2-3 candidate experiments, not committed solutions. Listing solutions at the decomposition stage locks in an answer before the problem is understood.
rule.rationale: Outcome roadmaps fail when they become feature roadmaps with outcome labels. Experiments must remain tentative until evidence promotes them.

rule.statement: Every outcome must include an "evidence we have" line and a "what we still need to learn" line. Confidence=high requires existing shipped data, not just a strong hypothesis.
rule.rationale: Confidence inflation — defaulting to "medium" or "high" without evidence — is the primary failure mode of outcome roadmaps. The evidence requirement makes confidence a checkable claim.

rule.statement: Maintain one source-of-truth roadmap. Generate four audience views from it: theme-based (customers, no dates, no internal metrics), outcome-metric (board, quarterly targets, confidence column), outcome-plus-options (engineering, sprint-level when known), problem-solution (sales, only confirmed items get timing).
rule.rationale: Four independent roadmap documents diverge immediately. Views generated from a single source remain synchronized and each line is traceable to a source row.

rule.statement: Customer-facing view stays at theme level for at least 2 quarters out ("better collaboration", not "real-time presence v2"). No internal metrics appear in the customer view.
rule.rationale: Specific feature names in customer-facing roadmaps create commitment expectations the team may not be able to meet. Theme-level language preserves flexibility while communicating direction.

rule.statement: Sales view is the only view that mentions specific solutions, and only for items at high confidence. The other three views must not name solutions for items below high confidence.
rule.rationale: Sales commitment to a specific feature creates a contractual obligation in customer minds. Naming solutions only for high-confidence items limits the blast radius of delivery misses.

rule.statement: Run the leading-indicator monitor weekly and propose promote/demote/replace actions to the PM. Quarterly, re-decompose the full goal tree. Re-version the roadmap only on real signal, not on every metric blip.
rule.rationale: Rewriting the roadmap on every data point destroys stakeholder trust. The weekly/quarterly cadence separates tactical adjustments from strategic re-decomposition.

rule.statement: Pair every roadmap presentation with a kill criterion: "if X does not move by Y, we drop this outcome." Forces honest stakes and prevents zombie outcomes.
rule.rationale: Outcomes without kill criteria become permanent features of the roadmap regardless of evidence. An explicit kill criterion makes the outcome falsifiable.

antipattern.description: Outcomes assigned confidence=medium or confidence=high without cited evidence. Agent defaults to "medium" because it sounds reasonable.
antipattern.reason: Uncalibrated confidence is opinion. Every confidence assertion must reference a query, dashboard URL, or an explicit "TO BE INSTRUMENTED" flag. Without it, the confidence column provides false precision that misleads planning.

rule.statement: Use exactly three confidence levels: low, medium, high. More levels create false precision. Confidence=high requires shipped data on the surface; confidence=medium requires some data or reasonable analogues; confidence=low is hypothesis only.
rule.rationale: A 5-level scale devolves into splitting hairs between "medium-high" and "high." The 3-level scale forces clean categorization.

antipattern.description: An audience view is edited directly rather than regenerated from the source of truth, causing the four views to diverge and contradict each other.
antipattern.reason: Direct edits to views break the single-source principle. When board and engineering views disagree, stakeholders lose confidence in all four. Run a daily diff job to flag lines in views without a source-of-truth trace.

antipattern.description: A business goal decomposed into 6-8 outcomes because every stakeholder's priority gets included.
antipattern.reason: More outcomes = less focus = slower experiments = longer time-to-learn. The hard cap at 4 exists precisely because stakeholder pressure tends toward expansion. A PM must own the decomposition decision and defend the cap.

antipattern.description: Sales pressure causes specific features and dates to appear in the board view or customer view for items that are below high confidence.
antipattern.reason: Sales pressure is a political problem, not a roadmap format problem. Outcome framing reduces but does not eliminate it. Solving it requires agreement on which view each audience receives and enforcement of view boundaries by the PM.

rule.statement: Board-facing roadmap leads with the metric movement, not the activity list. If a board member asks "where is the chat feature?", redirect to the outcome metric the feature is meant to move.
rule.rationale: Boards skim metrics and ask about named features because named features feel concrete. Leading with the metric reframes the conversation to impact, not output.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: A decomposition agent takes one business goal and produces 2-4 candidate product outcomes with leading indicators and an experiment menu, plus an honest "we don't know" branch when evidence is weak. A second agent generates the audience views (theme-based for customers, outcome-metric for board, options-with-confidence for engineering, problem-solution for sales) from the same source. A third agent monitors the leading indicators on a weekly cadence and proposes "promote/demote/replace" actions to the human PM. Human owns final decomposition and audience-view sign-off.

list.item: An outcome-decomposition agent (Opus) — strategy work; needs the highest reasoning model.
An audience-rewriter agent (Sonnet) — reformats one source-of-truth into 4 views without losing fidelity.
A leading-indicator monitor agent — pulls metrics weekly, flags drift greater than 10%.
faion-mlp-impl-planner-agent — once an outcome is committed, plans experiments under it.

section.name: tools-and-services
section.p: CLI tools for outcome roadmap automation: dbt (define and version leading-indicator metrics with lineage), metabase or superset API (programmatic dashboard creation per audience view), mermaid-cli (render outcome-tree diagrams), pandoc (convert one source markdown into 4 audience PDFs/HTML), gh project (manage outcome roadmap as GitHub Projects with custom fields).

Services (SaaS/OSS, agent-friendly): ProdPad (outcome-roadmap UX is best-in-class; "now/next/later" with confidence), Airfocus (configurable view per audience from one source), Productboard (strong customer-feedback linkage; audience-specific portals), Aha! Roadmaps (multiple "views" per roadmap is native), Jira Plans / Advanced Roadmaps (engineering-execution audience view; weak for outcome semantics), Notion + dbt + Hex (DIY outcome roadmap that integrates metric layer; cheap and flexible), OpenProject (self-hostable; supports custom outcome fields).

list.item: LLMs love to invent plausible-but-wrong leading indicators. Require that every metric has a query/dashboard URL or a flag of "TO BE INSTRUMENTED".
Outcome decomposition tends toward 6-8 outcomes per goal; force the agent to a hard cap of 4. More than 4 = lack of focus.
Audience-rewriter agents drift from source over time; run a daily diff job that flags lines in views without traceability.
Confidence inflation: agents default to "medium" because it sounds safe. Force evidence-based justification per confidence assertion.
Human-in-loop checkpoint: outcome trees are strategic; a human PM owns the final decomposition before any audience view is generated.
Human-in-loop checkpoint: customer-facing view requires human edit before publication — agent-generated phrasing leaks internal jargon.
Do not let the agent rewrite the roadmap on every metric blip. Cadence: weekly metric monitor, quarterly re-decomposition, only re-version on real signal.

section.name: source-of-truth-schema
section.p: The source-of-truth roadmap is one YAML file (`roadmap.yaml`) with stable row ids. All audience views must trace each printed line back to a row id. The schema below is the minimum every row must satisfy; the validator script enforces it.
section.code:
goal: statement: "Reduce 90-day churn from 18% to 12% by Q4 2026" metric: "churn_rate_d90" current: 0.18 target: 0.12 due: "2026-12-31"
outcomes: - id: O1 statement: "New users reach the 'aha' moment within first session" leading_indicator: name: "first_session_aha_pct" observable_within_days: 28 query_url: "https://metabase.example.com/question/142" current: 0.34 target: 0.55 confidence: medium evidence_we_have: - "Funnel data shows 34% reach feature X in session 1 (q142)" what_we_still_need_to_learn: - "Whether tooltip variant lifts feature-X first-touch" candidate_experiments: - id: O1.E1 title: "Inline tooltip on first empty state" status: "candidate" - id: O1.E2 title: "Sample-data prefill for new accounts" status: "candidate" kill_criterion: "If first_session_aha_pct does not exceed 0.40 by 2026-06-30, drop O1"

commands.command: git tag -a roadmap-v$(date +%Y.%m).$(git rev-list --count HEAD) -m "real-signal re-version"

content.prompt:
You are an outcome-decomposition agent. Decompose ONE business goal into 2-4 product outcomes.
INPUTS - business_goal: <one sentence with metric + target + date> - current_metrics: <list of accessible metrics with query URLs> - evidence_pool: <links to past experiment results, surveys, dashboards>
HARD RULES - Exactly 2-4 outcomes. Refuse to output 1 or 5+; ask the user to collapse or focus. - Each outcome MUST have: * statement in user-impact voice (NOT feature voice) * leading_indicator with observable_within_days <= 28 * evidence_we_have (1+ items, each with a real URL or "TO BE INSTRUMENTED") * what_we_still_need_to_learn (1+ items) * 2-3 candidate_experiments tagged status="candidate" * confidence in {low, medium, high}; high requires shipped data; medium requires "some data or reasonable analogue"; low is hypothesis only * kill_criterion (specific metric, threshold, date) - Add an explicit "we_dont_know" branch if evidence is weak. - Output STRICT YAML matching the source-of-truth schema. No prose outside YAML.

You are an audience-rewriter. Read roadmap.yaml and produce four files:
views/customers.md — theme-level only; no dates closer than current quarter; no internal metric names; no solution names; >= 2 quarters stay theme-level (e.g. "better collaboration"). views/board.md — outcome-metric table: outcome | metric | current | target | due | confidence. Lead each section with the METRIC, not the activity list. views/engineering.md — outcome + options menu + sprint window when an experiment has status=committed; status=candidate items show NO date. views/sales.md — problem-solution view. Mention a specific solution ONLY when its parent outcome confidence == "high" AND the experiment status == "committed". Otherwise refer to the outcome theme only.
Every printed line MUST end with a hidden HTML comment <!-- src:<row_id> --> referencing the source-of-truth row. Lines without a row id will be flagged and rejected by the drift-checker. Do not invent rows.

checklist.check: Goal is one sentence with a number and a date.
Outcome count is 2, 3, or 4 — never 1 or 5+.
Every outcome's leading indicator has observable_within_days within 28 days.
Every confidence value cites a query URL, dashboard, or explicit "TO BE INSTRUMENTED".
Every outcome has 2-3 candidate experiments at status=candidate.
Every outcome has a kill_criterion with metric, threshold, and date.
Source-of-truth file (roadmap.yaml) exists and validates against schema.
All four views regenerate from source; no view edited by hand.
view_drift.py reports zero drift before publication.
Customer view contains no internal metric names and no specific solution names.
Sales view names a solution only when parent outcome confidence == high AND experiment status == committed.
Board view leads with metric movement, not feature names.
Human PM has signed off on the outcome decomposition before any view is published.
Customer view has been human-edited before publication.
Weekly leading-indicator monitor is scheduled and routes promote/demote/replace proposals to PM.

verify.step: Run `python templates/validate-outcome-tree.py roadmap.yaml`. Expect exit 0 and zero violations on cap (2-4), evidence presence, confidence-evidence pairing, and 2-3 experiments per outcome.

Run the drift-checker `python tools/view_drift.py --source roadmap.yaml --views views/ --fail-on-drift`. Expect exit 0. Any flagged line means an audience view drifted from source — regenerate, do not patch.
Confirm the customer view contains zero occurrences of internal metric names: `grep -nE 'first_session_aha_pct|churn_rate_d90' views/customers.md` must return empty.
Confirm the sales view names solutions only for confidence=high committed items. Cross-check against `roadmap.yaml` with `python tools/check_sales_view_solutions.py views/sales.md roadmap.yaml`.
Confirm cadence is wired: `crontab -l | grep leading_indicator_monitor` returns a weekly entry, and a quarterly re-decomposition reminder exists in the team calendar.
Confirm a human-PM sign-off marker exists: `roadmap.yaml` has `signed_off_by:` field with a name and an ISO date within the last 90 days.

Success criteria for the methodology output: validator PASS, drift PASS, audience boundaries PASS, sign-off present, cadence scheduled. If any of the five fails, the methodology has not been applied — block publication.

template.purpose: Python validator: fails outcomes missing leading indicators, high confidence without evidence, or fewer than 2 experiments.
